title: Arnow Test for Catecholate-Type Siderophores
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e5lsk","text":"Please contact Dr. Steven Wilhelm (wilhelm@utk.edu) for additional information regarding this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ap5rm","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8nlj7","text":"Adapted from Arnow, L. E. Colorimetric determination of components of 3,4-dihydroxyphenylalanine-tyrosine mixtures. J Biol Chem 118, 531-537 (1937).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":116,"length":11},{"style":"bold","offset":128,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Bring 100 µL siderophore sample to 1 mL final volume with Milli-Q H 2 O
Add 1 mL of reagent 1 (0.5 N HCl) and mix undetermined
Add 1 mL of reagent 2 (10 g NaNO 2 , 10 g Na MO in 10 mL ultrapure, Chelex-100 treated H 2 O) and mix undetermined Omit this step for a blank sample.
Add 1 mL of reagent 3 (1 M NaOH) and mix undetermined
Add 1 mL H 2 O and mix undetermined
Allow 10-40 min for color reaction, and read in spectrophotometer at 500 nm 00:40:00
For a standard curve, use DHBA (dihydroxybenzoic acid)
creator: Steven W Wilhelm
